Title: Glutathione contributes to plant defence against parasitic cyst nematodes

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected roots at 10 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induction impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1; normal/stronger in rax1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as glutathione-dependent defence/camalexin pathway marker whose induction is compromised in glutathione-deficient mutants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTF6 is induced during the migratory stage of nematode infection in Col-0 roots. 2. This induction is impaired in glutathione-deficient mutants pad2, cad2, and zir1. 3. The result supports disruption of glutathione-linked defence metabolism in susceptible mutants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a defence marker; mediates conjugation between indole-3-acetonitrile and glutathione.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected roots at 10 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induction impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1; normal or stronger in rax1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Basal defence marker induction depends on adequate glutathione status during infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR1 transcript increases upon cyst nematode infection in Col-0. 2. This increase is compromised in glutathione-deficient susceptible mutants. 3. Adequate glutathione is required for proper defence signalling activation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene only; not directly manipulated.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with defence signalling during nematode infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected roots at 10 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induction impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1; normal or stronger in rax1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene-related defence marker induction is compromised under glutathione depletion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS2 is induced during early cyst nematode infection in Col-0. 2. The induction is impaired in glutathione-deficient susceptible mutants. 3. This supports defective defence signalling when glutathione levels are low.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene only; not directly manipulated.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with defence signalling during nematode infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected roots at 10 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induction impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1; normal or stronger in rax1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JA-related defence marker induction is compromised in glutathione-deficient susceptible mutants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ10 is induced during early infection in Col-0 roots. 2. The induction is impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1. 3. Adequate glutathione is required for proper activation of jasmonate-linked defence responses during infection.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene only; not directly manipulated.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected roots at 10 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induction impaired in pad2, cad2, and zir1; normal/stronger in rax1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indole-glucosinolate defence pathway activation is compromised in glutathione-deficient susceptible mutants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81F2 is strongly induced upon early cyst nematode infection in Col-0. 2. Induction is impaired in glutathione-deficient susceptible mutants. 3. This supports a role of glutathione in activating indole-glucosinolate-related defence metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as defence pathway marker rather than directly tested causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Col-0-derived allelic series including pad2, cad2, zir1, rax1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; mutants pad2, cad2, zir1, rax1
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamate-cysteine ligase; first enzyme of glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent ligation of glutamate and cysteine to form γ-glutamylcysteine, the first step in glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool; required for biosynthesis of glutathione that supports defence-associated camalexin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, especially nematode infection sites during migratory stage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redox buffering and defence against cyst nematodes; supports activation of defence pathways and camalexin production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots during migratory stage (10 hpi) and sedentary stage (10 dpi) of Heterodera schachtii infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected root segments and syncytia-containing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterodera schachtii infection; migratory stage 10 hpi and sedentary stage 10 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced-function alleles lowering glutathione generally increase susceptibility to cyst nematode and impair defence/camalexin induction; rax1 is an exception with reduced syncytium size and stronger camalexin induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as first and focal biosynthetic step affecting glutathione pool and defence outcome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allelic series infection assays; RT-qPCR; HPLC quantification of glutathione, cysteine, and camalexin; redox imaging with Grx1-roGFP2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptomic data from prior studies were mined for GSH1/GSH2 expression; no accession provided in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSH1 expression is significantly induced during both migratory and sedentary stages of cyst nematode infection. 2. GSH1 mutant alleles pad2, cad2, and zir1 show enhanced susceptibility, including more females and more eggs per cyst. 3. GSH1 mutants fail to raise glutathione upon infection and show impaired induction of defence marker genes and camalexin biosynthetic responses. 4. Adequate GSH1-dependent glutathione is required for effective defence against nematode infection.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe loss of function in GSH1 is embryo-lethal or causes strong root-growth defects (background information for null/rml1); partial depletion compromises defence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing GSH1-dependent glutathione biosynthesis may improve resistance to cyst nematodes, but excessive disruption of glutathione homeostasis causes developmental penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focused on Arabidopsis alleles pad2, cad2, zir1, and rax1; rax1 behaved differently from other alleles despite reduced glutathione.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redox buffering and defence-associated metabolism during nematode infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during migratory stage (10 hpi) but unchanged during sedentary stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increases early after infection, suggesting contribution to glutathione biosynthesis during defence, but no direct mutant functional analysis in Results
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Public transcriptomic survey and RT-qPCR validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptomic data from prior studies were mined for GSH2 expression; no accession provided in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSH2 is induced at the migratory stage of cyst nematode infection. 2. GSH2 is not induced at the sedentary stage. 3. The paper did not functionally dissect GSH2 beyond expression profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although experimentally measured, GSH2 was not the primary subject of downstream functional analysis; included because it is directly investigated in Results as a glutathione biosynthesis gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the conversion of dihydrocamalexic acid to camalexin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots at nematode infection sites during migratory stage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defence metabolite contributing to resistance against cyst nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in infected roots at 10 hpi in Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Impaired induction in pad2, cad2, and zir1; stronger/normal induction in rax1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterodera schachtii infection at migratory stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAD3 induction correlates with glutathione-dependent camalexin accumulation and reduced nematode susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream defence-associated step for camalexin production in this context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of infected root segments; HPLC camalexin profiling interpreted with GSH1 mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD3 is strongly induced upon early cyst nematode infection in wild-type roots. 2. This induction is impaired in glutathione-deficient mutants pad2, cad2, and zir1. 3. Reduced PAD3 induction is associated with lower infection-induced camalexin accumulation and higher susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports engineering strategies that maintain glutathione status to preserve PAD3-mediated camalexin defence against nematodes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAD3 is discussed as a key camalexin biosynthesis gene, but it is a downstream readout rather than the main manipulated gene in this study.
